text: Great question! You're already in the right place — the **Setup tab** generates platform-specific code for your WordPress site. Here's what you'll see on the Setup tab: **The tracking pixel** monitors which AI bots visit your site. [1] FlowBlinq GEO provides two implementation options: 1. **HTML image tag** — Add to your page's ``: ```html ``` 2. **Script tag (recommended)** — Add to your page's ``: ```html ``` This script automatically injects JSON-LD schema blocks when AI bots visit. [1] **For WordPress specifically:** The Setup tab should show you the exact code with your site slug filled in. You'll typically add it to your theme's footer file or use a code injection plugin. If the Setup tab doesn't clearly show WordPress-specific instructions, email **hello@flowblinq.ai** and they can walk you through the quickest method for your setup. Once added, your site will start tracking which AI crawlers (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini) visit your pages. ✨ Need help with any of the other setup steps?
